globalDisclaimer: Not a diagnosis — just a rough map from how you answered today. Your real life is messier than five sliders.
confidenceNote: If everything looks extreme, slow down and retake when you're tired-honest, not story-honest.

low.summary: You probably like what works. New angles can feel like noise until they prove themselves.
strengths: You ship instead of spiraling in hypotheticals
You keep teams from chasing shiny objects
You notice when an idea won't survive contact with reality
blindSpots: You might shut the door on something useful because it feels unfamiliar
Curiosity can read as skepticism even when you're just being careful
low.contextCaveat: Under pressure, you tend to double down on the known — which is often smart, until the world moves.

typical.summary: You can get curious without needing every week to feel like a lab experiment.
strengths: You toggle between imagination and execution
You enjoy novelty when it has a point
You don't need constant stimulation to stay engaged
blindSpots: Sometimes you underfeed the creative side, sometimes the practical one — depends on the season
People may misread you as either "safe" or "weird" depending on the day
typical.contextCaveat: This one swings with boredom, stress, and who you're around. That's normal.

high.summary: Ideas pull you. You notice layers other people scroll past — and you can get restless when life feels too small.
strengths: You connect dots across domains
You learn for the pleasure of it
Ambiguity doesn't automatically panic you
blindSpots: Follow-through can lag when the new thing arrives
You may romanticize novelty and undervalue maintenance
high.contextCaveat: Works beautifully in exploratory roles; grinds in places that hate change.

low.summary: You'd rather improvise than live inside a spreadsheet. Structure helps — until it feels like a cage.
strengths: You adapt fast when plans collapse
You're comfortable with "good enough" when perfection would slow everyone down
You bring energy when things are ambiguous
blindSpots: Deadlines can sneak up when no one's watching
Small dropped threads pile up quietly
low.contextCaveat: This often spikes when you're interested, and slips when you're not. That's the whole trick.

typical.summary: You can plan — you just don't want your life to become the plan.
strengths: You finish what matters without turning everything into a system
People can usually count on you in normal weeks
You know when to tighten and when to loosen
blindSpots: Heavy load or chaos can collapse your scaffolding fast
You might blame discipline when it's actually bandwidth
typical.contextCaveat: Stress is the real test here, not your best Tuesday.

high.summary: You like things finished, clear, and where they belong. Loose ends itch.
strengths: You carry long projects across the finish line
People trust you with detail and follow-through
You create calm by reducing entropy
blindSpots: Perfectionism can steal the win
Pivoting late hurts more for you than for others
high.contextCaveat: High standards help until they become the voice that never clocks out.

low.summary: People can drain your battery even when you like them. You recharge alone — and that's not a flaw.
strengths: You think before you talk
You listen without needing to fill silence
Solo focus is actually your superpower
blindSpots: Your good ideas may arrive quietly — easy to miss in loud rooms
Networking-style small talk can feel like unpaid labor
low.contextCaveat: You can show up socially when you need to; it just doesn't always refill you.

typical.summary: You're not a hermit and not a spotlight-seeker — you're situational, which confuses people who want a simple label.
strengths: You can join the room without needing to own it
You recover from overstimulation if you get space
You pick depth over volume when it matters
blindSpots: People read you differently in different contexts — that inconsistency isn't fake, it's human
You might overcommit socially, then crash
typical.contextCaveat: Energy here depends on sleep, stakes, and whether you actually want to be there.

high.summary: Talking it out helps you think. Stillness can feel like waiting — not resting.
strengths: You warm up rooms
You initiate when others hesitate
Momentum is easier for you to create than to fake
blindSpots: You might talk past the quiet processors
Stillness can feel like a problem to solve
high.contextCaveat: High stimulation roles fit; deep solo craft still needs guardrails.

low.summary: You're willing to be the person who says the uncomfortable thing. Harmony is nice — but not always honest.
strengths: You push back when groupthink shows up
You negotiate without folding early
You protect boundaries when softness would cost you
blindSpots: Warmth doesn't always read on your face
Your directness can land harder than you meant
low.contextCaveat: This can be a strength in hard decisions — and friction in fragile relationships.

typical.summary: You can cooperate without disappearing. You can disagree without scorching earth.
strengths: You look for workable compromises
You care how people feel — within reason
You can switch between soft and firm when the situation asks
blindSpots: Under pressure you might go too accommodating — or too sharp
You might resent harmony you paid for with silence
typical.contextCaveat: The middle is flexible; track what you actually paid for peace.

high.summary: You feel other people's weather. You'd rather repair than win — until someone mistakes kindness for weakness.
strengths: You de-escalate tension people pretend isn't there
You build trust fast
You remember the human side of decisions
blindSpots: Hard truths get delayed
You may carry conflicts that were never yours
high.contextCaveat: Harmony is a gift until it trains people to ignore your needs.

low.summary: Stress shows up for you, but it doesn't usually hijack the wheel. That steadiness is rarer than it sounds.
strengths: You recover faster from rough patches
You don't catastrophize every ping on your phone
You can hold uncertainty without spinning
blindSpots: You might underestimate how shaken someone else is
Risk signals in relationships can look "dramatic" from your side
low.contextCaveat: Steady doesn't mean numb — check you're not dismissing real problems.

typical.summary: Some weeks you're fine; some weeks your mind runs hot. The line moves — that's the whole deal.
strengths: You notice problems early enough to fix them
You're not pretending life is easy
You can self-soothe when you have space
blindSpots: Sleep, caffeine, and overload change this fast
You might judge yourself for "overreacting" when you're just human
typical.contextCaveat: Context wins: same person, different month, different number.

high.summary: Your nervous system keeps a tighter grip on the steering wheel. The world feels louder than it "should."
strengths: You catch mistakes before they compound
You're attuned to emotional weather — yours and theirs
You don't need fake positivity scripts
blindSpots: Rumination can turn a small thing into a long night
Criticism echoes longer than you want it to
high.contextCaveat: This is tendency, not destiny — skills and support change the lived version a lot.
